user_message: I need to draft a response for the Contoso Ltd engagement. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 10:59 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Contoso Ltd

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 11:40 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 10:06 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-01-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), David (Contoso Ltd)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:05:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:15:00] David (Contoso Ltd): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:20:00] David (Contoso Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF14-B trap (Confuses influence with authority); produces complete Stakeholder map with roles (decision-maker, influencer, champion, blocker), recency labels, confiden
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF14-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF14-B failure mode (Confuses influence with authority)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF14-B -- Confuses influence with authority
